{{Short description|Rate of change of angular displacement; frequency multiplied by 2π}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Angular frequency
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Frequency]], [[Simple harmonic motion]], [[Wave]], [[Oscillation]]
| symbol = ω
| SI_unit = [[radian per second]] (rad/s)
| dimension = T⁻¹
}}

'''Angular frequency''' (or '''angular speed''') is a scalar measure of rotation rate. In physics, angular frequency <math>\omega</math> is the magnitude of the vector quantity [[angular velocity]]. The term is used for periodic motion such as [[circular motion]], [[oscillation]], and [[wave]] propagation.

Angular frequency is related to ordinary [[frequency]] <math>f</math> by:
:<math>\omega = 2\pi f</math>

The [[SI unit]] is [[radian per second]] (rad/s). Since radians are dimensionless, angular frequency has the same physical dimension as frequency, T⁻¹.

== Circular motion ==
For [[uniform circular motion]] with period <math>T</math>:
:<math>\omega = \frac{2\pi}{T} = 2\pi f</math>

The relation between linear speed <math>v</math> and angular frequency is:
:<math>v = \omega r</math>

== Simple harmonic motion ==
{{Main|Simple harmonic motion}}
In [[simple harmonic motion]], position varies sinusoidally:
:<math>x(t) = A\cos(\omega t + \phi)</math>

The angular frequency of a [[mass–spring system]]:
:<math>\omega = \sqrt{\frac{k}{m}}</math>

For a [[simple pendulum]] (small amplitude):
:<math>\omega = \sqrt{\frac{g}{l}}</math>

== Waves ==
For a travelling [[wave]]:
:<math>y(x,t) = A\sin(kx - \omega t)</math>

The angular frequency relates to [[wavenumber]] <math>k</math>:
:<math>\omega = vk</math>
where <math>v</math> is the [[phase velocity]].

== Quantum mechanics ==
In [[quantum mechanics]], energy and angular frequency are linked by:
:<math>E = \hbar\omega</math>
where <math>\hbar</math> is the [[reduced Planck constant]].

The [[time-dependent Schrödinger equation]] for stationary states:
:<math>\Psi(t) = \psi e^{-i\omega t}</math>

== LCR circuits ==
For an [[RLC circuit|LC circuit]], the resonant angular frequency is:
:<math>\omega_0 = \frac{1}{\sqrt{LC}}</math>

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Frequency]]
* [[Hertz]]
* [[Simple harmonic motion]]
* [[Wave]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Frequency]]
[[Category:Wave mechanics]]
[[Category:Physical quantities]]
